evals.prompt: I'm done working on the feature/add-user-auth branch. Commit the changes and create a PR.
evals.expected_output: Commits uncommitted changes, pushes, and creates a draft PR displaying the PR URL. Auto-detects the base branch and performs sensitive file checks.
expectations: Auto-detects the base branch and displays it to the user
Verifies CLI authentication with gh auth status
Checks for sensitive files such as .env
Stages specific files by name instead of using git add -A or git add .
Includes a Co-Authored-By trailer in the commit message
Pushes with git push -u origin HEAD
Creates the PR with gh pr create --draft
Passes the PR body via HEREDOC
Displays the PR URL and a change summary upon completion

evals.prompt: I modified .env.local and src/config.ts on this branch. Create a PR against main.
evals.expected_output: Warns about the .env.local file and does not commit it without user confirmation. Uses main as the base branch.
expectations: Uses main as the base branch as specified in $ARGUMENTS
Detects .env.local as a sensitive file and warns the user
Does not commit .env.local without user confirmation
Creates the PR with gh pr create --draft --base main

evals.prompt: Create a PR. All changes are already committed and pushed.
evals.expected_output: Skips commit and push steps since they are already done and proceeds directly to PR creation. Also checks for an existing PR.
expectations: Confirms no uncommitted changes via git status and skips the commit step
Detects the branch is already pushed and up-to-date with remote, skipping the push step
Checks for an existing open PR on the branch with gh pr list
Creates a new PR if none exists
Includes the PR URL in the completion report
